name: Shrimp-Gruyere Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Cream cheese, softened (8 oz. each)
1 1/4 cup Shredded Gruyere or Swiss cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine cracker crumbs and 1/4 cup melted butter. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Set aside.
2. Peel, devein, and chop shrimp. Cook shrimp and the next four ingredients in 3 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, for 4 to 5 minutes or until shrimp are done and vegetables are tender. Drain well and set aside.
3. Beat cream cheese and mayonnaise at high speed of an electric mixer until creamy. Add eggs, one at a time, beating after each addition. Gradually add milk, beating well at low speed just until blended. Stir in shrimp mixture, Gruyere cheese, and white pepper.
4. Pour the mixture into the prepared pan. Bake at 300°F for 1 hour and 20 minutes or until set. Turn off the oven and partially open the door; leave cheesecake in the oven for an additional hour.
5. Cool completely on a wire rack. Cover and chill at least 8 hours. Serve with warm Italian Tomato sauce. Garnish with sweet red pepper strips, whole shrimp, and fresh basil sprigs if desired.
Italian Tomato Sauce Directions:
1. Cook onion and garlic in oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until tender.
2. Add tomatoes and remaining ingredients; bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 20 minutes or until most of the liquid evaporates, stirring occasionally.
4. Remove and discard bay leaf.
